title: Chamaecytisus ciliatus
intro: Chamaecytisus ciliatus, also known as downy broom or red broom, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Chamaecytisus ciliatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-cancer activities. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and may be used to support digestive health and immune function, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
taxonomy: Chamaecytisus ciliatus is a plant species that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the tribe Genisteae and the genus Chamaecytisus. The genus Chamaecytisus is part of the subfamily Faboideae within the family Fabaceae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics. Chamaecytisus ciliatus is a flowering plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia.

The plant's leaves and stems are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations.
